summary: ContinueConversation continuity survives live-session loss: a code word stated in turn 1 is recalled in turn 2 after the tmux session is killed, via native --resume off the persisted handle
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.code_word_only_spoken: never written to disk — recall proves provider-native resume

agent_review.notes: CONTINUITY-AFTER-LOSS proven end to end (ids in output rotate per run; the shape/fingerprint is what's pinned). Turn 1 acknowledged the code word (turn1_answer="OK"); the LIVE tmux session (killed_tmux_session) was then destroyed — the run log confirms 'tmux session lost: can't find pane'; turn 2 auto-relaunched a fresh tmux and resumed the native session (native_session_id) via '🔁 Continuing claude-code with native session ...', and recalled the EXACT code word (code_word == turn2_recall, recalled_after_loss=true). The word was only ever spoken in-conversation, never written to disk, so recall across a destroyed live session isolates provider-native --resume as the sole mechanism — exactly the keep-alive→resume fallback ContinueConversation + the persisted handle own. Answer clean and coherent.
